channel.title: Comments for page "Anart Antics"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Anart Antics"

item.content:encoded: Hell yeah!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only interesting part of the article to me was the inclusion of Nobody, and even there it's because it's hinting at a bigger plot which the tale had yet to establish up to that point. The rest isn't up to my liking. I guess this type of story doesn't appeal to me much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very entertaining so far, and it's so nice to see trans characters who's attention isn't drawn to the fact that they're trans. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also got the Cool War reference. I kinda spazzed at the words "Hawaiian T-shirt".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trainers can call out their Pokémon any way they damned well please. I'm not a big fan of the anime and haven't watched it much, but I still can confirm that Ash has said both.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't it be: "Copyright Infringement, I choose you!" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has enough personality and character to make up for minor flaws. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I mentioned in the chat, this one amused me, and the interactions between the two artists managed to be lighthearted and fun without veering into excessively goofy territory. It's also, I think, a clever way to employ the bathtub, a skip which I am still sorting out my feelings over.

Two non-anomalous, non-AWCY?, non-asshole anartists.

Not why I upvoted, but Jesus Christ, thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm… well, there were bits I liked - mostly the Library bits, and the general overall tone and plot. But I hate the bathtub, and I didn't like the self-aware joke at the end, so for me it started and ended badly. I also don't know who Nobody is, so that part made very little sense to me. I assume it was him making them sleepy?

Edit: re-read it, and I decided the bits I dislike outweigh the bits I like, and so I'm going to downvote. Mostly because I can't stand that bathtub, and I don't want to see it win the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I, for one, kinda like the Nobody connection. Perhaps that's just me.

I upvoted at

"Man, this is one interesting book," she said, without a hint of sarcasm. That was the important bit. "I'm really glad I found it!"

Well done, Twisted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kinda like the Cool War tie-in (is no one recognizing this as Randomini's Nobody?), but I've got serious reservations about this.

The bathtub ends up being important in the long run, and I like tying this in with 2015, but it did seem be a long way from point A to point B, and I kind of started wondering what the point was in the middle.

I'm not liking the "the Library teaches anartists how to anart" explanation. Too easy.

And I was kind of enjoying the experience of "they made Blastoise lol" but then you had to go and make the terrible self-aware joke at the end, and that just totally killed the whole thing for me. I might not mind seeing other people do Cool War-style anart-centric tales in the future, even with these characters (though I can't say they left any strong impression on me), but this doesn't work, as far as I'm concerned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Too much happens for it to be entirely about the concept, and too little happens for it to be about the plot. Unfortunately, it feels like several pieces of a thing rather than one unified thing. It's okay, though. The prose isn't bad and the characterization (while a little flat) is competent.

I'll give a 0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this twice in draft form, so I saved it for last (TG already knows the content of my criticisms). I neutral vote for reasons that are, upon reflection, probably more personal than not—I don't really like the characterization of the two as quirky for quirky's sake, and their play-fighting interactions seem kind of saccharine. I'm down with "slice of life," but with these sorts of characterizations, the story needs to make me smile or give off some sort of charming feel, and I don't think it succeds.

(but maybe im just really grumpy)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This meanders a bit, but in a good way. I like the slice-of-anart-life-yness feel to it. I think the two main characters are kind of weak though.

I kind of like the Nobody inclusion as just a weird, out of the blue, one of thing that doesn't have any actual purpose. It fits the general feel of the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I, too, don't really see the point of the Nobody inclusion, but I enjoyed the rest of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems like this didn't really end, and I'm a bit confused if I should take it seriously or not. It carried on like comedic tale with a plot, but then it finished on an open-ended Pokemon reference. Everything before this was pretty interesting though. The creative parts were creative and the funny parts were actually funny. Honestly, I'm pretty confused on how I should feel about this. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Feast about Nobody being involved (he kinda just seems shoehorned in a lot), but aside from that, I personally don't think it dragged on too long. The ending was pretty awesome too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one a lot, and not just because you used one of my SCPs. It was very entertaining and funny, and I enjoyed all the references to SCP-2015. Nobody's cameo seemed unnecessary to me, but everything else was great. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't mind the length, although the first section might stand some pruning. Getting to see what the WAFC 'pits' feel like, the random and thoroughly confusing cameo by a certain nameless orange fruit, and the last exchange were all entertaining, and I'll upvote entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Originally I thought that this tale dragged on for a little to much, but the ending made it more than worth it. Congrats TG. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't believe I (in a jokingly pimpy way) just said "Use 1974" in chat after you asked for a skip to use along with the anart mechas, and you went with it. You wove an interesting story and represented the bathtub and the way I imagine people reacting to it perfectly.

+1, you've earned it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it initially, but it just dragged on a bit too long. No vote.

item.title: Author's Anecdotes About Anart Antics
item.content:encoded:
I've been meaning to write about these characters for a while now. Two non-anomalous, non-AWCY?, non-asshole anartists. It's rare, I know, but turns out they do exist. In case anyone didn't pick up on it, Aldon is (MtF) transgender. More to come when I get around to it.

The mechas come from GottaGoFeast's SCP-2015, which I knew I was writing about the moment the contest was announced. The bathtub is Devereaux's SCP-1974, at his request. I thought it was going to be difficult incorporating it, but after I realized it produced infinite water everything just clicked into place.

I had a lot of fun writing this, hope y'all like it.
